citeinfo.title: Assessing spatial variability of nutrients and related water quality constituents in the California Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta at the landscape scale: 2018 High resolution mapping surveys (ver. 2.0, October 2023)

Assessing spatial variability of nutrients, phytoplankton, and related water-quality constituents in the California Sacramento–San Joaquin Delta at the landscape scale—2018 high resolution mapping surveys

descript.abstract:
The dataset documents the spatial and temporal variability of nutrients and related water quality parameters at high spatial resolution in the North Delta, Central Delta, and the Western Delta out to Suisun Bay in the Sacramento-San Joaquin River Delta of California, USA. The dataset includes nitrate, ammonium, phosphate, dissolved organic carbon, temperature, conductivity, dissolved oxygen, and chlorophyll as well as information about phytoplankton community composition. Data-collection cruises were conducted under three different environmental/flow conditions in May, July, and October of 2018. The data release consists of a xml document, 13 text/csv documents, and a zip file. Descriptions for each document and file are listed below:
1. METADATA: (2018_High_resolution_mapping_surveys_v2.0.xml) – Metadata for child item “Assessing spatial variability of nutrients and related water quality constituents in the California Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta at the landscape scale: 2018 High resolution mapping surveys.” 2. QA DATA: (Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Corrections and Offsets.csv) – Data corrections and offsets calculations applied to the datasets. 3. DATA DICTIONARY (Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Dictionary v2.0.csv) – Data dictionary for tables and attributes. 4. DISCRETE SAMPLING DATA: (Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Discrete Sampling v2.0.csv) – Discrete sampling, water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 5. PHYTOPLANKTON ENUMERATION DATA FROM DISCRETE SAMPLES: (Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Discrete Sampling Phytoenumeration v2.0.csv) – Discrete sampling, phytoenumeration data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 6. May 2018 FIELD MAPPING DATA (High resolution, timestamped and 20 second median filtered): (Delta Water Quality Mapping May 2018 High Resolution.csv) – High resolution, in situ, unfiltered, in vivo fluorescence (IVF), water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. All measurements were collected in the field. 7. May 2018 MAPPING DATA ON COMMON SPATIAL FRAMEWORK: (Delta Water Quality Mapping May 2018 Spatially Aligned.csv) – Spatially Aligned (Centerline Extracted) and interpolated, water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 8. July 2018 FIELD MAPPING DATA (High resolution, timestamped and 20 second median filtered): (Delta Water Quality Mapping July 2018 High Resolution.csv) – High resolution, in situ, unfiltered, in vivo fluorescence (IVF), water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. All measurements were collected in the field. 9. July 2018 MAPPING DATA ON COMMON SPATIAL FRAMEWORK: (Delta Water Quality Mapping July 2018 Spatially Aligned.csv) – Spatially Aligned (Centerline Extracted) and interpolated, water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 10. FIELD MAPPING DATA (High resolution, timestamped and 20 second median filtered): (Delta Water Quality Mapping October 2018 High Resolution.csv) – High resolution, in situ, unfiltered, in vivo fluorescence (IVF), water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. All measurements were collected in the field. 11. October 2018 MAPPING DATA ON COMMON SPATIAL FRAMEWORK: (Delta Water Quality Mapping October 2018 Spatially Aligned.csv) – Spatially Aligned (Centerline Extracted) and interpolated, water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 12. CALCULATED DIFFERENCES BETWEEN MAY AND JULY, 2018: (Delta Water Quality Mapping May to July 2018 Differences.csv) – Calculated differences between months for water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 13. CALCULATED DIFFERENCES BETWEEN MAY AND OCTOBER 2018: (Delta Water Quality Mapping May to October 2018 Differences.csv) – Calculated differences between months for water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 14. CALCULATED DIFFERENCES BETWEEN JULY AND OCTOBER, 2018: (Delta Water Quality Mapping July to October 2018 Differences.csv) – Calculated differences between months for water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 15. SHAPE FILES FOR ALL RESULTS: (Maps_Shapefiles.zip) – Zip file containing 117 shapefiles for spatially aligned water quality constituents from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. 16. METHODS: (Methods_ChildItem_v2.0.pdf) – Text document describing methods used for data and sample collection and analysis.
descript.purpose: The objective of this project is to document the variability of nutrients and related water quality parameters at high spatial resolution in the North Delta, Central Delta, and the Western Delta out to Suisun Bay (California, USA). This data release reports measurements that include nitrate, ammonium, phosphate, dissolved organic carbon, temperature, conductivity, dissolved oxygen, chlorophyll, blue-green algal pigments, and phytoplankton community structure.
descript.supplinf: See files: Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Corrections and Offsets.xlsx; Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Dictionary v2.0.xlsx for further information regarding the datasets.

idinfo.useconst: Although this data set has been used by the U.S. Geological Survey, U.S. Department of the Interior, no warranty expressed or implied is made by the U.S. Geological Survey as to the accuracy of the data and related materials. The act of distribution shall not constitute any such warranty, and no responsibility is assumed by the U.S. Geological Survey in the use of this data, software, or related materials. Any use of trade, product, or firm names is for descriptive purposes only and does not imply endorsement by the U.S. Government.
idinfo.native: Environment of Metadata Creation: Microsoft Windows 10 Enterprise Version 1803 (OS Build 17134.885); Esri ArcGIS 10.6.1 (Build 9270) Service Pack N/A (Build N/A)

attracc.attraccr: EXO sondes were calibrated before use in the field with the KorEXO software. For calibration, the sonde sensors are submerged one at a time in standards, then the verified chemical concentration is recorded within the instrument. If the instrument measures an unknown value of the parameter, it refers to the calibration information to calculate the concentration of the chemical in the water (EXO User Manual, pg. 81).

dataqual.logic: Redundancy is built into the system, meaning many of the parameters included in the data set are measured by 2 different instruments. Data was displayed in real-time for on the spot user verification. Topology rules were applied to points, lines, and polygons to ensure the integrity of the geospatial data.
dataqual.complete: Data set is considered complete for the information presented, as described in the abstract. Users are advised to read the rest of the metadata record carefully for additional details.

horizpa.horizpar: We conducted a visual check that the GPS data we recorded matched the boat GPS. Data was also displayed in real-time, allowing us to spot check GPS data underway. In addition, GPS location information was compared to field notes that described known locations and features.

vertacc.vertaccr: We conducted a visual check that the GPS data we recorded matched the boat GPS. Data was also displayed in real-time, allowing us to spot check GPS data underway. In addition, GPS location information was compared to field notes that described known locations and features. Datum used NAD1983.

procstep.procdesc:
Broad area water quality mapping surveys of the Sacramento-San Joaquin River Delta (California, USA) were conducted three times in 2018 during the months of May, July, and October using the USGS R/V Mary Landsteiner, a 26-foot (~8 meter) long motorboat. For each of these months, transects were conducted on three successive days: May 15, 16, and 17; July 24, 25, and 26; and October 17, 18, and 19. Each day covered one of three regions of the Delta: North (~100 km), Central (~120 km), and South (~100 km). On each day data were collected during daylight hours from approximately 07:30 am to 3:30 pm pacific standard time (PST).
For analysis, sample water was continuously pumped onto the boat while underway at speeds up to 30 mph (13 m/s) using a pick- up tube mounted at a fixed depth of approximately 1 m below the surface, routed through a screen to remove large debris and into a pressure-compensated manifold that maintained system pressure at a proscribed level irrespective of boat speed (Downing et al., 2016). A 2-stage debubbler was used to remove bubbles that can interfere with optical measurements. Sample water was split into separate flow paths for each analytical flow path, with constant flow rates maintained by metering the discharge line, except for the flow path serving the open split interface, which was metered on the feed line. Flows were continuously monitored using a sight gauge for each flow path.
More detailed methods documentation can be found on the landing page (https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/5d3f1ae2e4b01d82ce8d90e0).

Discrete water samples were collected either underway or–in the case of more extensive sample requirements–while stopped on station. Discrete samples were collected while underway either using a fourth flow path on the sampling manifold or using a separate system with a separate pick-up tube mounted adjacent to the main sampling pick-up and pumping sample through a 0.45-micron filter into a sample bottle. Samples were collected while underway approximately every 2 miles, principally for determination of ortho-phosphate (PO4). At designated sampling stations (approximately 10 per day) samples for determination of NH4, NO3, PO4, dissolved organic carbon (DOC), and total dissolved nitrogen (TDN) concentrations were collected using this system. Additionally, unfiltered samples were collected approximately from 1 m depth using a submersible pump for analysis of chlorophyll-a (Chl-a), pheophytin, phycocyanin, allophycocyanin, and phycoerythrin pigments, phytoenumeration, and picocyanobacterial counts. Samples for phytoplankton enumeration and picocyanobacteria counts were fixed immediately with Lugol’s iodine solution (4 mL) and formaldehyde (1 mL), respectively.
More detailed methods documentation can be found on the landing page (https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/5d3f1ae2e4b01d82ce8d90e0).

Data from onboard continuous instruments not directly logged to the flow-through data collection system were merged based on timestamp to the nearest second. All data directly logged to the flow- through data collection system was processed using pandas software library in Python to remove periods of compromised data (e.g. flow blockages, bubbles), to apply instrument corrections and unit conversions, and to apply a centered 20 second median filter to the time series. Final NO3 values were obtained by regressing instrument response against NO3 concentrations obtained from laboratory measurements of discrete samples collected through the course of the day. Individual sample results with more than three standard deviations from the regression were judged to be outliers and removed from the regression. NH4 data was processed by first correcting the voltage output of the instrument for baseline drift based on the instrument response during periodic measurements of organic-free deionized water (n ≥10) and then using a regression model of the voltage response to a standard concentration. A regression coefficient of >0.97 R² or better was judged to be acceptable. The NH4 data timestamp was corrected to account for the time lag associated with the microfluidic processing prior to integration with other data sources.
Data was spatially aligned (SA) to a common spatial framework to facilitate comparisons between dates and to minimize the effects of differential data density on the spatial interpolation calculations by assigning median-filtered data by proximity to a centerline vector comprising points located at the centerline of all channels navigated, spaced at approximately 150 m intervals. Interpolated water quality maps were created from the spatially aligned data using ArcGIS Spline with Barriers tool (Terzopoulos and Witkin, 1988). The raster output was smoothed with the Focal Statistics tool, and interpolated values were extracted to the spatially aligned points to create continuous spatially aligned data across the mapping domain.
More detailed methods documentation can be found on the landing page (https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/5d3f1ae2e4b01d82ce8d90e0).

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping May 2018 High Resolution.csv (May 2018 HR)
enttyp.enttypd: High resolution, in situ, unfiltered, in vivo fluorescence (IVF), water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. All measurements were collected in the field.

attr.attrdef: Date and time (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss), pacific standard time

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

Ammonium, micromolar

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

attr.attrdef: Bluegreen algae contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with bluegreen algae by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent

attr.attrdef: Diatoms, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping May 2018 Spatially Aligned.csv (May 2018 SA)
enttyp.enttypd: Spatially Aligned (Centerline Extracted) and interpolated, water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area.

attr.attrdef: Centerline point identifier, see metadata for details
Sampling time period, month, year
GSC NAD 1983, decimal degrees
GSC NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Name of the approximate location for center line points

attrdomv.udom: Name of the approximate location for center line points

attr.attrdef: Ammonium, micromolar
Nitrate plus nitrite, micromolar
Orthophosphate, micromolar; interpolated, see metadata for details
Total dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) concentration calculated as the sum of nitrate, nitrite and ammonium ([NO3] + [NO2] + [NH4]), micromolar
Dissolved organic nitrogen, micromolar; modeled based on fDOM, see metadata for details
Total dissolved nitrogen, micromolar; modeled, see metadata for details
Dissolved organic carbon, milligrams per liter; modeled based on fDOM, see metadata for details
Ratio of nitrate to ammonium, calculated as [NO3]/[NH4], molar to molar

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

attr.attrdef: Contribution of ammonium (NH4) to the dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) pool, calculated as 100*[NH4]/[DIN], percent

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

attr.attrdef: Contribution of nitrate (NO3) to the dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) pool, calculated as 100*[NO3]/[DIN], percent

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

attr.attrdef: Ratio of dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) to orthophosphate, calculated as [PO4]/[DIN], molar to molar

edom.edomvd: Empty cell denotes no data available.

attr.attrdef: Contribution of dissolved organic nitrogen (DON) to the total dissolved nitrogen (TDN) pool, calculated as 100*[DON]/[TDN], percent
Ammonium concentration slope expressed as angles, degrees
Nitrate concentration slope expressed as angles, degrees
Orthophosphate concentration slope expressed as angles, degrees
Conductivity, microsiemens per centimeter
Dissolved oxygen, percent of saturation
Dissolved oxygen, milligrams per liter
Fluorescence of dissolved organic matter, quinine sulfate units (1 QSU = 1 part per billion quinine sulfate); corrected for turbidity and inner filter effects
pH, standard units
Salinity, practical salinity unit
Specific conductance, microsiemens per centimeter at 25 degrees Celcius
Temperature, degrees Celsius
Turbidity, formazin nephelometric units
Fluorescent chlorophyll-a, micrograms per liter; corrected for temperature
Total chlorophyll-a, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter
Bluegreen algae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Bluegreen algae contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with bluegreen algae by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Cryptophyceae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Cryptophytes contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with cryptophytes by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Diatoms, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Diatoms/dinoflagellates contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with diatoms by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Green algae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Green algae contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with green algae by the sum of green algae, bluegreen, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Transmission, percent
Yellow substances correction, micrograms per liter

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Discrete Sampling PhytoEnumeration v2.0.csv

attr.attrdef: Date and time (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss), pacific standard time

attr.attrdef: Informal field identifier associated with numeric station code

attrdomv.udom: Informal field identifier associated with numeric station code

NAD 1983, decimal degrees
NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Phytoplankton taxonomy division name

attrdomv.udom: Phytoplankton taxonomy genus name

attrdomv.udom: Phytoplankton taxonomy genus name

attr.attrdef: Phytoplankton taxonomy species name

attrdomv.udom: Phytoplankton taxonomy species name

attr.attrdef: The numbers of cells counted in a sample
The number of cells per volume, cells per milliliter
The biovolume of a specific taxa in a sample, cubic micrometers per milliliter

enttyp.enttypd: Shapefile table containing attribute information associated with the data set.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.

attrdomv.udom: Coordinates defining the features.

attr.attrdef: Minimum parameter value within a contour.
Maximum parameter value within a contour.
Name identifier used to join tables in Tableau.

attrdomv.udom: Name identifier used to join tables in Tableau.

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping July 2018 High Resolution.csv (July 2018 HR)
enttyp.enttypd: High resolution, in situ, unfiltered, in vivo fluorescence (IVF), water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. All measurements were collected in the field.

attr.attrdef: Date and time (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss), pacific standard time
Garmin GPS16X-HVS, NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Garmin GPS16X-HVS, NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Ammonium, micromolar
Nitrate plus nitrite, micromolar
Conductivity, microsiemens per centimeter
Dissolved oxygen, percent of saturation
Dissolved oxygen, milligrams per liter
Fluorescence of dissolved organic matter, quinine sulfate units (1 QSU = 1 part per billion quinine sulfate); corrected for turbidity and inner filter effects
Fluorescent dissolved organic matter, quinine sulfate equivalents; corrected for temperature, turbidity, and inner filter effect
pH, standard units
pH, millivolts
Salinity, practical salinity unit
Specific conductance, microsiemens per centimeter at 25 degrees Celcius
Temperature, degrees Celsius

Beam transmissometer/attenuation at 650 nm, per meter

Cryptophytes contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with cryptophytes by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent

Transmission, percent

Temperature, degrees Celsius

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping July 2018 Spatially Aligned.csv (July 2018 SA)
enttyp.enttypd: Spatially Aligned (Centerline Extracted) and interpolated, water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area.

attr.attrdef: Centerline point identifier, see metadata for details
Sampling time period, month, year
NAD 1983, decimal degrees
NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Name of the approximate location for center line points

attrdomv.udom: Name of the approximate location for center line points

attr.attrdef: Ammonium, micromolar
Nitrate plus nitrite, micromolar
Orthophosphate, micromolar; interpolated, see metadata for details
Total dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) concentration calculated as the sum of nitrate, nitrite and ammonium ([NO3] + [NO2] + [NH4]), micromolar
Dissolved organic nitrogen, micromolar; modeled based on fDOM, see metadata for details
Total dissolved nitrogen, micromolar; modeled, see metadata for details
Dissolved organic carbon, milligrams per liter; modeled based on fDOM, see metadata for details
Ratio of nitrate to ammonium, calculated as [NO3]/[NH4], molar to molar
Contribution of ammonium (NH4) to the dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) pool, calculated as 100*[NH4]/[DIN], percent
Contribution of nitrate (NO3) to the dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) pool, calculated as 100*[NO3]/[DIN], percent
Ratio of dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) to orthophosphate, calculated as [DIN]/[PO4], molar to molar
Contribution of dissolved organic nitrogen (DON) to the total dissolved nitrogen (TDN) pool, calculated as 100*[DON]/[TDN], percent
Ammonium concentration slope expressed as angles, degrees
Nitrate concentration slope expressed as angles, degrees
Orthophosphate concentration slope expressed as angles, degrees
Conductivity, microsiemens per centimeter
Dissolved oxygen, percent of saturation
Dissolved oxygen, milligrams per liter
Fluorescence of dissolved organic matter, quinine sulfate units (1 QSU = 1 part per billion quinine sulfate); corrected for turbidity and inner filter effects
pH, standard units
Salinity, practical salinity unit
Specific conductance, microsiemens per centimeter at 25 degrees Celcius
Temperature, degrees Celsius
Turbidity, formazin nephelometric units
Fluorescent chlorophyll-a, micrograms per liter; corrected for temperature
Total chlorophyll-a, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter
Bluegreen algae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Bluegreen algae contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with bluegreen algae by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Cryptophyceae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Cryptophytes contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with cryptophytes by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Diatoms, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Diatoms/dinoflagellates contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with diatoms by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Green algae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Green algae contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with green algae by the sum of green algae, bluegreen, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Transmission, percent
Yellow substances correction, micrograms per liter

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping October 2018 High Resolution.csv (October 2018 HR)
enttyp.enttypd: High resolution, in situ, unfiltered, in vivo fluorescence (IVF), water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area. All measurements were collected in the field.

attr.attrdef: Date and time (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss), pacific standard time
Garmin GPS16X-HVS, NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Garmin GPS16X-HVS, NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Ammonium, micromolar
Nitrate plus nitrite, micromolar
Conductivity, microsiemens per centimeter
Dissolved oxygen, percent of saturation
Dissolved oxygen, milligrams per liter
Fluorescence of dissolved organic matter, quinine sulfate units (1 QSU = 1 part per billion quinine sulfate); corrected for turbidity and inner filter effects
Fluorescent dissolved organic matter, quinine sulfate equivalents; corrected for temperature, turbidity, and inner filter effect
pH, standard units
pH, millivolts
Salinity, practical salinity unit
Specific conductance, microsiemens per centimeter at 25 degrees Celcius
Temperature, degrees Celsius
Temperature, degrees Celsius
Turbidity, formazin nephelometric units
Beam transmissometer/attenuation at 650 nm, per meter
Transmittance, percent
Fluorescent chlorophyll-a, micrograms per liter; corrected for temperature
Fluorescent chlorophyll-a, micrograms per liter; corrected for temperature
Total chlorophyll-a, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter
Bluegreen algae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Bluegreen algae contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with bluegreen algae by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Cryptophyceae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Cryptophytes contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with cryptophytes by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Diatoms, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Diatoms/dinoflagellates contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll-a, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with diatoms by the sum of green algae, bluegreen algae, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Green algae, micrograms of chlorophyll-a per liter

Green algae contribution to the total conc of chlorophyll, estimated by dividing the micrograms per liter of chlorophyll-a associated with green algae by the sum of green algae, bluegreen, diatoms, and cryptophyta, percent
Transmission, percent
Yellow substances correction, micrograms per liter
Temperature, degrees Celsius

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping July to October 2018 Differences.csv (July to October 2018 DIFF)
enttyp.enttypd: Calculated differences between months for water chemistry data from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta study area.

attr.attrdef: Center line point identifier; see metadata for details
Month and year used for difference calculations

edom.edomvd: Month and year used for difference calculations

attr.attrdef: NAD 1983, decimal degrees
NAD 1983, decimal degrees
Name of the approximate location for center line points

attrdomv.udom: Name of the approximate location for center line points

attr.attrdef: Ammonium difference, micromolar
Nitrate difference, micromolar
Orthophosphate difference, micromolar
Dissolved inorganic nitrogen difference, micromolar
Total dissolved nitrogen difference, micromolar

Month and year used for difference calculations

edom.edomvd: Month and year used for difference calculations

NAD 1983, decimal degrees

attrdomv.udom: Name of the approximate location for center line points

attr.attrdef: Ammonium difference, micromolar
Nitrate difference, micromolar
Orthophosphate difference, micromolar
Dissolved inorganic nitrogen difference, micromolar
Total dissolved nitrogen difference, micromolar

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Corrections and Offsets.csv
enttyp.enttypd: Data corrections and offsets calculations applied to the datasets.

attr.attrdef: Type of correction applied.

attrdomv.udom: Type of correction applied.

attr.attrdef: Equation used for the correction.

attrdomv.udom: Equation used for the correction.

attr.attrdef: The instrument which corrections were applied to.

attrdomv.udom: The instrument which corrections were applied to.

attr.attrdef: Reference used for the correction method

edom.edomvd: Reference used for the correction method

enttyp.enttypl: Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Dictionary v2.0.csv
enttyp.enttypd: Data dictionary for tables and attributes.

attr.attrdef: Abbreviations used for labels and instruments

attrdomv.udom: Abbreviations used for labels and instruments

attr.attrdef: Description of abbreviations and attributes

attrdomv.udom: Description of abbreviations and attributes

attr.attrdef: Instrument and equipment detection range

attrdomv.udom: Instrument and equipment detection range

attr.attrdef: Equation to obtain specific conductance

overview.eaover: The data dictionary is titled Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Dictionary v2.0. This file lists and describes the labels in tables. The files with "High Resolution" on their title are used with an Excel macro to create the files with "Spatially Aligned" on their title. This files are then incorporated into ArcGIS Pro to interpolate the data and create the prediction maps. See methods for more details.
overview.eadetcit: The files are available on the landing page: https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/5d3f1ae2e4b01d82ce8d90e0.

cntaddr.address: Denver Federal Center, Building 810, Mail Stop 302

distinfo.distliab: Although this data set has been used by the U.S. Geological Survey, U.S. Department of the Interior, no warranty expressed or implied is made by the U.S. Geological Survey as to the accuracy of the data and related materials. The act of distribution shall not constitute any such warranty, and no responsibility is assumed by the U.S. Geological Survey in the use of this data, software, or related materials. Any use of trade, product, or firm names is for descriptive purposes only and does not imply endorsement by the U.S. Government.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
